title: campanula scheuchzeri subsp. pollinensis
intro: Campanula scheuchzeri subsp. pollinensis, also known as Alpine Bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Campanulaceae family, native to the European Alps. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Campanula scheuchzeri subsp. pollinensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
